steps.title: Find the Opening Thesis
steps.content: The passage opens with an explicit statement: “We are creatures of the sun. We love light and life. That is why we crowd into the towns and cities.” The author is identifying a human trait and directly linking it to a choice about where people live—the core purpose of the text.

steps.title: Match the Purpose to the Choices
steps.content: Choice B describes exactly what the text does: identify human traits (love of light, fear of darkness/solitude) that motivate urban living. The other choices fundamentally misread the passage: A and D reverse which environment isolates people, and C attributes a caution the text never makes. That matches choice B — B is correct.
